# Class Feature Implementation Audit

**Date:** 2026-07-09
**Method:** Static evidence only (compendium + data + consumer inspection). **No Foundry runtime execution.**
**Machine data:** `docs/audits/class-feature-implementation-status.json` (generated by `tools/check-feature-implementation-coverage.mjs`).

> **Addendum (2026-07-10) — class-type coverage reinterpretation.** Coverage is measured **by class
> role**, not as a single `n/37`. **Base classes** are the character's starter chassis and own the
> starting package (starting feats/proficiencies, class skills, starting credits); **prestige
> classes** are additive specialization layers and do **not** provide a starting package. Evidence:
> base **5/5 (100%)** carry `class_skills`/`starting_features`/`starting_credits`; prestige **32/32**
> carry the additive fields (`talent_trees`/`level_progression`/`babProgression`/`defenses`). The
> earlier `5/37` numerator conflated the two roles. Prestige start-package emptiness is **by design**
> and must not be "fixed" by giving prestige classes base-class starter packages. The tooling now
> emits split `baseFieldCoverage`/`prestigeFieldCoverage` with a per-field expectation.

## Inventory

- **37 heroic classes** in `packs/classes.db`: **5 base** (Scoundrel, Noble, Scout, Soldier, Jedi) + **32 prestige/special**.
- **434 entries** in `packs/nonheroic.db` (NPC-oriented nonheroic blocks).
- **`beasts.db`** and follower templates exist separately (granted via talents/level per project rules).
- **`data/class-features.json`** — data-first registry of **53 class-feature abilities** with `executionModel`/`abilityMeta` (same mechanism as feats).
- **Prestige prerequisites** live in **`data/prestige-class-prerequisites.json` (32 entries — full coverage)**, not in `classes.db`.

## Per-feature coverage (populated / 37) and consumer status

| Class feature | Populated | Consumer | Bucket | Notes |
|---------------|-----------|----------|--------|-------|
| Hit die / `base_hp` | 37/37 | `derived-calculator` / HP calculator | **B** | HP progression path exists |
| `babProgression` | 37/37 | `bab-calculator` / derived | **B** | |
| `defenses` | 37/37 | `defense-calculator` | **B** | Class defense bonuses |
| `level_progression` (+ typed features) | 37/37 | progression engine | **B** | Prestige features live here (`talent_choice`, `force_technique_choice`, `class_feature`) |
| `talent_trees` / `talentTreeIds` | 37/37 | ProgressionContentAuthority / talent-tree-membership-authority | **B** | Verify per-source visibility (see risks) |
| `grants_force_points` | 31/37 | progression / force-points | **B** | 6 classes don't grant FP (plausible) |
| `class_skills` | **base 5/5 · prestige n/a** | chargen | **B (base)** | Base classes own class skills. Prestige `class_skills=[]` is **by design** (additive PrC skills, not a starter list) — see addendum |
| `trainedSkills` (count) | **base 5/5 · prestige n/a** | chargen | **B (base)** | Base-class starter package; prestige not expected |
| `starting_features` | **base 5/5 · prestige n/a** | progression | **B (base)** | Prestige features are in `level_progression`, so empty here is **by design** |
| `starting_credits` | **base 5/5 · prestige n/a** | chargen | **B (base)** | Base-class starter package; prestige not expected |
| Prestige entry prerequisites | 32/32 (separate file) | `prerequisite-checker` / `prestige-readiness` | **B** | Data complete; verify enforcement at finalization |

## Base classes (Scoundrel, Noble, Scout, Soldier, Jedi)

Data-complete: HP, BAB, defenses, class skills, trained-skill count, starting feats/features, starting credits, talent trees, level progression. **Strongest class implementation — verify → A.** These are the right first runtime-verification target (create one of each, confirm HP/BAB/defenses/class-skills/talent access at level 1).

## Prestige classes (32)

- **Features are present** via `level_progression[].features` (typed `class_feature` / `talent_choice` / `force_technique_choice`). Verify the progression engine consumes each type (esp. `force_technique_choice` / `force_secret`).
- **`class_skills` is empty for all prestige classes — this is expected, not a base-package gap.** Prestige classes are additive layers; they do not carry a starter class-skill list the way base classes do. In SWSE a PrC may add a small number of class skills on top of the existing build. So the correct question is the *additive* one: **do a prestige class's own class skills (where the source grants any) add correctly to the character?** — a low-priority runtime verification, NOT a data backfill. Do not populate prestige `class_skills` with a base-class-style list.
- **Prerequisites are data-complete** (32/32 in `prestige-class-prerequisites.json`) and there is a `prerequisite-checker` + `prestige-readiness` path. **Verify the checker actually blocks entry when unmet** (fail-closed), rather than only advising.

## Known-sensitive areas (explicitly requested)

| Area | Static finding | Bucket | Action |
|------|----------------|--------|--------|
| **Galaxy of Intrigue Noble talent tree visibility** | Noble (base) has full `talentTreeIds`; GoI trees registered via talent-tree-membership-authority | **B / verify** | Runtime-check the GoI Noble trees appear in the talent picker for a Noble |
| **Jedi Knight Force technique / secret** | Jedi Knight `level_progression` has `force_technique_choice` entries; ForceRegistry + force-technique-engine exist | **B / verify** | Runtime-check technique/secret choices are offered and applied |
| **Nonheroic fallback** | 434 nonheroic blocks; player nonheroic not normally selectable | **P (by design)** | Keep punted; verify NPC nonheroic derive HP/BAB/defenses |
| **Class resolution canonical & fail-closed** | `ClassesRegistry.resolveModel()` returns `null` on unknown/failure (no fabricated fallback); ClassesRegistry = enumeration authority, ProgressionContentAuthority = seam, class-data-loader = legacy adapter | **B (good)** | Confirm callers treat `null` as fail-closed, not silently continue |

## Fully-automated candidates (verify → A)

HP, BAB, and defense progression for all 37 classes have concrete derived-calculator paths. Base-class chargen (skills/feats/credits) is data-complete. These are the highest-confidence class features.

## Partial / needs decision

- **Prestige `class_skills` emptiness** — reclassified: **by design**, not a gap. Residual = verify additive PrC class-skill resolution where a source grants any (low priority).
- **Prestige prerequisite enforcement** — data exists; verify it *blocks*, not just advises.
- **`class-features.json` (53 abilities)** — verify the applier consumes them like feat metadata (executionModel/abilityMeta), or they're descriptive-only.

## Recommended next batches (classes)

1. **Runtime-verify the 5 base classes** end-to-end (HP/BAB/defenses/class-skills/talent access).
2. **Verify prestige prerequisite enforcement** is fail-closed at progression finalization.
3. **Verify additive prestige class-skill resolution** (only where a PrC source grants class skills). Do **not** fill prestige `class_skills` with base-class-style starter lists — empty is by design.
4. **Verify Jedi Knight force technique/secret** and **GoI Noble talent tree** visibility/application.

## Limitations

Static only. Field population ≠ runtime application; "consumer exists" is inferred from imports/reads, not execution. All B/C rows need a Foundry pass to confirm.
